methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Wardstone, a Seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Sebastian and Tobias Fischer, has raised $5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a broader comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though direct peer comparables at Wardstone's stage remain sparse.

A stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, using $85M as a total-raised proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement in the sector. This yields an implied valuation of $212M. Peer-median triangulation is not feasible given insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Space & Aerospace Seed cohort (only 1 adjacent-stage peer identified against a 3-datapoint minimum threshold). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples—the sector median trades at 42.9x across 6 public tickers—cannot be applied without Wardstone's revenue, which is not disclosed.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $181M to $244M, with a median of $212M. This range rests on a single triangulation method, introducing material uncertainty. Key risks include the synthetic nature of the base-value proxy and the lack of transaction comparables at the early Seed stage. Revenue disclosure and additional peer transactions would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
